title: Proposal for a scheduler support library
body:
**Work in progress**, to be discussed in the design meeting on June 9.

This PR would lay the groundwork for a library of static schedulers in `circt/Support/Scheduling` (please suggest better locations).

The scheduling problems faced by HLS-like synthesis flows are often similar. The idea here is to have a hierarchy of base classes that model these variants and take care of storing and validating the problem and its solutions. Currently, only the basic problem is modeled (class `Scheduler`). Variants (e.g. "pipelined", "resource-sharing", etc.) would subclass it, add additional properties for the operations, dependences, operator types or the problem itself, and provide appropriate verification methods.

A demo client is the `-schedule-pipeline` pass, which schedules the operations inside a `staticlogic.pipeline` with unit latencies and annotates the computed start times as attributes. (This could be removed from this PR if desired -- I included this pass mainly to test the interface).

`ASAPScheduler` is a complete implementation that solves the basic problem.

Note that this is the follow-up to #1040, focussing more on the library-of-schedulers aspect, with a CIRCT-agnostic interface. The discussion of the earlier PR showed that there is no obvious way that conversion passes from a untimed to a timed dialect would want to provide the information needed for scheduling (OpInterface, Attributes, ...), so better abstractions will need to evolve over time, inside concrete synthesis flows.
